summary: Test-only addition (report/report_record_statio_test.go + 3 goldens) is correct and high quality. The 16-col stat_io and 10-col stat_io_time layouts match internal/query/io.go (Ncols/DiffIntvl/UniqueKey/OrderKey) exactly, diff math in the goldens is verified, and the file follows the established sibling-test harness pattern. Tests pass and go vet is clean; no critical or major findings.

suggestions.suggestion: statIOAnsiRE/statIOStripANSI duplicate the ansiRE/stripANSI helpers already defined in report_record_replslots_test.go (same package). They are intentionally re-prefixed to avoid a redeclaration conflict while keeping the new file self-contained and parallel-safe across Wave 2 tasks, but this leaves two identical ANSI helpers in the package. Once Wave 2 files are merged, consider consolidating into a single shared test helper (e.g. report_record_helpers_test.go) to remove the duplication.
suggestions.benefit: Removes a small DRY violation and a second source of truth for ANSI stripping in the report test package.

suggestions.suggestion: wantRows are asserted via assert.Contains on the whitespace-collapsed output. This is robust here because each expected row is prefixed by a unique io_key (k_client01 / k_bgwrit01 / k_wal00001), so substring collisions are impossible and the trailing exact-golden comparison pins full ordering. No change required; noting only that Contains-based row checks rely on the unique-prefix invariant, which is safe as written.
suggestions.benefit: Documents why the membership-style assertion is sufficient alongside the golden equality check.
